Name: dynamic_mixed_precision_quantization
Title: Dynamic Mixed-Precision Quantization for Latency-Aware Inference
Short Hypothesis: Dynamic adaptation of mixed-precision quantization based on real-time latency constraints can significantly enhance the efficiency of machine learning models during inference, while maintaining accuracy. This approach is necessary given the increasing complexity of models and the demand for real-time performance in latency-sensitive applications.
Related Work: Existing mixed-precision frameworks typically rely on static or heuristic-based methods that do not adapt to real-time latency demands. For instance, KVTuner and DQMQ focus on layer-wise quantization but fail to incorporate runtime latency constraints effectively. Our proposal distinguishes itself by dynamically adjusting quantization schemes in response to real-time inference latency, leveraging insights from both model sensitivity and hardware capabilities.
Abstract: In the era of complex machine learning models, maintaining efficient inference while adhering to strict latency requirements is critical, especially for edge devices. This proposal introduces a novel framework for dynamic mixed-precision quantization that adapts quantization levels in real-time based on latency feedback from the inference process. We propose utilizing a combination of sensitivity analysis and runtime latency monitoring to inform quantization decisions. By implementing a feedback loop that adjusts precision on-the-fly, we can optimize resource utilization and model performance simultaneously. This approach not only minimizes latency overhead but also maintains model accuracy across diverse operational contexts. Comprehensive empirical evaluations will be conducted to demonstrate the effectiveness of our framework, with a focus on achieving optimal throughput-accuracy trade-offs. Our results will pave the way for more efficient, scalable machine learning deployment in latency-critical applications.
Experiments: Implement a baseline mixed-precision quantization model for comparison.
Develop a dynamic quantization algorithm that adjusts precision based on latency feedback.
Test the performance of both static and dynamic quantization models across various hardware settings.
Evaluate metrics including latency, accuracy, throughput, and memory usage to assess the efficacy of the dynamic approach.
Conduct ablation studies to isolate the impact of different components of the dynamic quantization framework.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The dynamic adjustment mechanism may introduce computational overhead that could negate latency benefits.
Real-time monitoring and adjustment may be sensitive to hardware variability.
The framework's performance could be heavily reliant on the accuracy of the latency prediction model.
